title: I Do the Daily Things Wealthy Women Do
subtitle: An affirmation for anchoring daily wealth-building habits as a current identity
excerpt: A short affirmation from Day 56 of a 12-week money program. The statement names daily wealth-building practices in present tense, not as aspirations, but as what is already in place.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 56 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

content.text: A short affirmation for anchoring the daily actions that build wealth as current practice. The statement is present tense: not a plan, not a future intention, but what is already happening.
I do the daily things wealthy women do. I save, I plan, I keep calm with money. Small consistent actions build the wealthy life I am creating.

Say it out loud three times, hand on the centre of your chest. If specific daily habits come to mind as you say it, let each register as evidence, not as a to-do list: the savings transfer, the morning check-in, the bill paid on time.
Adapted from Day 56 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
